MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-20-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, MAR-20-2026 RE: PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. THE INTERESTED PARTIES MADE APPEARANCES IN PERSON. THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE WAS NO PROPER NOTICE CONTESTING THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING THEREFORE, THE COURT DOES NOT CALL THE MATTER AND ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER SARAH LEWIS SEEKS A WRIT OF MANDATE TO COMPEL THE SMALL CLAIMS DIVISION OF THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT TO RULE ON HER REQUEST FOR RECONSIDERATION. THE WRIT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO LEWIS'S NOTICING HER HEARING IN THE PROPER DEPARTMENT. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: REAL PARTY IN INTEREST ENA REALES' Motion To Quash Service Of Writ Of Mandate.

The interested parties made appearances in person. The court notes that there was no proper notice contesting the court's tentative ruling therefore, the court does not call the matter and adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar as untimely filed and served.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

PETETION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

The interested parties made appearances, in person. The court notes that there was no proper notice contesting the court's tentative ruling therefore, the court does not call the matter and adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Sarah Lewis seeks a writ of mandate to compel the Small Claims Division of the San Francisco Superior Court to rule on her request for reconsideration. The writ is denied without prejudice to Lewis's noticing her hearing in the proper department. Lewis has noticed the writ for hearing in Department 301 of this court, a law and motion department. A petition for writ of mandate relating to a small claims action other than one that relates to a post judgment enforcement order "may be heard by a judge who is assigned to the appellate division of the superior court" (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 116.798, subd. (a)(1)), or "by the court of appeal or by the Supreme Court" (id., subd. (a)(2)). The law and motion department is not designated as an appellate division. Lewis must notice her writ for hearing in the appellate division of the superior court. The request for writ is denied without prejudice.

Because the court is not reaching the merits of the writ petition, it does not pass on the argument raised in real party's motion to quash that service was not proper.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
